videos.title: Tally Marks | How to Count Using Tally Marks
videos.description: Learn how to use tally marks to count and record data quickly. Every 5th mark goes across to make counting easy. Practice tally marks with fun examples!
learningObjectives: Draw tally marks for any number
Read tally marks to find the count

videos.title: Collecting and Organising Data | Data Handling for Kids
videos.description: See how collected data is organised into tally charts and frequency tables. Watch step-by-step examples of turning survey results into neat tally tables!
learningObjectives: Create tally charts from raw data

videos.title: Counting and Sorting Objects | Sorting into Groups
videos.description: Practice counting and sorting with tally marks. Sort objects into groups and use tally marks to record how many are in each group.
learningObjectives: Apply tally marks to sort and count
Record sorted data using tally marks
Check counts by reading tally marks back

videos.title: Pictographs for Kids | Reading and Making Picture Graphs
videos.description: See how tally chart data can be turned into pictographs. This video bridges tally marks and pictographs, showing how one leads to the other.
learningObjectives: Understand the connection between tallies and pictures

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Quick tally marks and transition to pictographs for strong students

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness
ready_performance.rationale: Complete coverage of tally marks with practice

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Start with counting review, then tally marks

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites

questions.question: In a tally chart, how many does |||| ||| stand for?
questions.explanation: |||| is a group of 5, and ||| is 3 more. So |||| ||| = 5 + 3 = 8.

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: Have students practise tally marks by doing a live survey in class. Ask a question like 'What is your favourite season?' and record answers using tally marks on the board.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: At home, ask your child to count items using tally marks. For example, tally the number of each type of vehicle they see from the window in 10 minutes.
usageNotes.studentGuidance: Tally marks are a quick way to count! Draw a line for each thing you count, and every 5th line goes across the group. It makes counting super easy!
